title: Sunday thali notes
dek: A warm vegetarian thali review focused on balance, service and the little details that make a meal memorable.
body: Swati looks for food that feels generous without being heavy. This thali had the right rhythm: bright chutneys, soft breads, fragrant dal and a dessert that ended the meal gently rather than loudly. The strongest point was balance — spice, texture and service all worked together.

title: Brunch with a view
dek: A bright cafe visit with good coffee, thoughtful plating and a few notes on value.
body: The best brunch places understand pacing. The Garden Table gets the atmosphere right first: soft light, calm service and plates that arrive looking cared for. The coffee was excellent, the toast was crisp, and the only miss was a slightly long wait at peak time.

title: Street food favourite
dek: Bold flavours, quick service and a stall worth returning to.
body: This was the kind of street food that makes a short lunch feel like a discovery. The chaat was layered, crunchy and bright; the team moved fast without making the experience feel rushed. Great value and full of personality.
